title: Path traversal in legacy load_prompt config loaders in langchain-core
description: The legacy prompt loaders in langchain_core.prompts.loading (load_prompt / load_prompt_from_config) read files from paths embedded in a deserialized config dict. In _load_template(), a path taken from the config key `{var_name}_path` (e.g. template_path, suffix_path, prefix_path) is passed directly to pathlib.Path(...).read_text() with only a file-extension check (.txt) and no validation against absolute paths or `..` traversal sequences. Sibling helpers _load_examples() and _load_few_shot_prompt() have the same flaw for .json/.yaml example files. An application that feeds an untrusted or user-influenced prompt config to these APIs lets an attacker read arbitrary files on the host (constrained only by extension), e.g. template_path='/tmp/secret.txt' or '../../etc/secret.txt'.
